theme: Seed a cellular automaton to grow a target
title: Grow the Glyph
seed_problem: seed a deterministic totalistic CA to hit a target pattern at step T
mutation: composite: the CA is a radius-2 LINEAR (XOR) rule, so the whole T-step map is a fixed GF(2) linear operator; the seed budget forces solvers to find a low-weight representative inside the operator's solution coset (its null space = an exploitable dynamical invariant) rather than any single feasible preimage
strategies: trivial: seed nothing (the all-zero fixed point) -> reproduces the checker's do-nothing baseline
naive greedy: seed exactly the cells that are live in the target, budget-capped, and hope the rule leaves it alone -- the mixing rule scrambles it within a few steps

insight (strong): rebuild the single-step update as an NxN matrix over GF(2), obtain the T-step operator by repeated squaring, solve the linear system by Gauss-Jordan elimination to get a particular precursor plus a null-space basis, then use the null space (the rule's invariant directions -- seed changes invisible to the T-step outcome) to trade cost down until the precursor fits the budget
a general solver could go further: search for a MINIMUM-weight coset representative (exact algebraic dual of the budget constraint) instead of a first feasible one
